title: Constitution Enforced
description: The Constitution of India came into force on January 26, 1950, laying down the supreme legal framework of the republic.
sscImportance: Foundational static GK fact tested regularly in Tier-1.

title: First Constitutional Amendment
description: Introduced the Ninth Schedule to protect land reform laws from judicial review and added reasonable restrictions to freedom of speech under Article 19.
sscImportance: Very High. Creation of the Ninth Schedule is a high-yield exam favorite.

title: 24th Amendment
description: Affirmed Parliament's power to amend any part of the Constitution (including Fundamental Rights) and made presidential assent to amendment bills mandatory.
sscImportance: High. Re-established parliamentary supremacy after the Golaknath verdict.

title: 25th Amendment
description: Restricted property rights and prioritized specific Directive Principles (Article 39b and c) over Fundamental Rights under Article 31C.
sscImportance: High. Important for tracing the evolution of DPSP vs Fundamental Rights friction.

title: Kesavananda Bharati Judgment
description: The Supreme Court delivered its historic 13-judge bench ruling propounding the 'Basic Structure Doctrine', restricting Parliament from tampering with core constitutional features.
sscImportance: Maximum. One of the most frequently asked legal landmark cases in SSC CGL history.

title: National Emergency Declared
description: An internal emergency was proclaimed across India under Article 352, leading to major suspension of civil liberties and political detentions.
sscImportance: High. Crucial historical context for emergency provisions and subsequent amendments.

title: 42nd Constitutional Amendment
description: Famously called the 'Mini-Constitution', it added 'Socialist, Secular, Integrity' to the Preamble, created Part IVA (Fundamental Duties), and altered judicial powers.
sscImportance: Maximum. Heavily scrutinized in questions due to its vast structural changes.

title: 44th Constitutional Amendment
description: Enacted by the Janata government to undo 42nd Amendment excesses; removed Right to Property as a Fundamental Right and replaced 'internal disturbance' with 'armed rebellion'.
sscImportance: Maximum. Essential for understanding modern emergency safeguards and property rights shifts.

title: 52nd Amendment
description: Added the Tenth Schedule to the Constitution, establishing the Anti-Defection Law to check political horse-trading.
sscImportance: Very High. Direct questions on Anti-Defection and Tenth Schedule details.

title: 61st Amendment
description: Amended Article 326 to lower the voting age for Lok Sabha and Legislative Assembly elections from 21 years to 18 years.
sscImportance: High. A classic static GK question for general awareness sections.

title: 73rd Amendment
description: Gave constitutional status to Panchayati Raj Institutions by adding Part IX and the Eleventh Schedule (29 matters).
sscImportance: Maximum. Core staple topic for local self-government questions.

title: 74th Amendment
description: Gave constitutional status to Urban Local Bodies (Municipalities) by adding Part IX-A and the Twelfth Schedule (18 matters).
sscImportance: Maximum. Frequently paired alongside the 73rd amendment in matching tests.

title: 86th Amendment
description: Made elementary education a fundamental right under Article 21A for children aged 6 to 14, and introduced the 11th Fundamental Duty.
sscImportance: Very High. Article 21A and education rights are top-tier exam priorities.

title: 91st Amendment
description: Capped the size of the Council of Ministers at 15% of the total house strength and tightened anti-defection provisions.
sscImportance: High. The 15% ministry rule is a favorite for polity quizzes.

title: 101st Amendment
description: Introduced the Goods and Services Tax (GST) regime and established the GST Council under Article 279A.
sscImportance: Very High. Essential current affairs-polity crossover question area.

title: 102nd Amendment
description: Granted constitutional body status to the National Commission for Backward Classes (NCBC) under Article 338B.
sscImportance: High. Important for tracking constitutional commissions.

title: 103rd Amendment
description: Introduced 10% reservation in education and public employment for Economically Weaker Sections (EWS) via clauses in Articles 15 and 16.
sscImportance: High. Frequently tested regarding EWS quota legal basis.

title: 104th Amendment
description: Extended SC/ST reservation deadlines in Lok Sabha and state assemblies to 2030 while discontinuing Anglo-Indian nomination seats.
sscImportance: High. Noted particularly for the discontinuation of Anglo-Indian seats.

title: 105th Amendment
description: Restored the explicit power of state governments to identify and maintain their own state lists of Socially and Educationally Backward Classes.
sscImportance: Medium-High. Recent amendment frequently tested in updated exam papers.
